name: Gltf

unions.name: Error
unions.comment: All possible failures

unions.name: Gltf
unions.comment:
Internal state. Your application model should contain one:

    type alias Model =
        { gltf : Gltf
        }

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment:
Internal Msg type. Handle these in your [update](Gltf#update) function. Your application Msg type should contain:

    type Msg
        = GltfMsg Gltf.Msg

unions.name: Query
unions.comment: Type returned by [queries](Gltf#queries).

unions.name: QueryResult
unions.comment: Results from a [query](Gltf#queries) which can be used to retrieve [content](Gltf#content).

aliases.name: Asset
aliases.type: { version : String.String, copyright : Maybe.Maybe String.String, generator : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

values.name: defaultSceneQuery
values.comment: Queries the glTF file for the default scene. If no default scene is defined this will query the first scene.

values.name: getBinary
values.comment:
Get default scene from a file of type **.glb**

**Note:** If file does not specify a default scene this returns the first in list

values.name: getBinaryWithQuery
values.comment:
Get content from a file of type **.glb** by supplying one of following queries:

  - [Gltf.defaultSceneQuery](Gltf#defaultSceneQuery) (same as using [getBinary](Gltf#getBinary))
  - [Gltf.sceneQuery](Gltf#sceneQuery)

values.name: getGltf
values.comment:
Get default scene from a file of type **.gltf**

**Note:** If file does not specify a default scene this returns the first in list

values.name: getGltfWithQuery
values.comment:
Get content from a file of type **.gltf** by supplying one of following queries:

  - [Gltf.defaultSceneQuery](Gltf#defaultSceneQuery) (same as using [getGltf](Gltf#getGltf))
  - [Gltf.sceneQuery](Gltf#sceneQuery)

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initialize a [Gltf](Gltf#Gltf) and store it in your model:

    init : Model
    init =
        { gltf = Gltf.init
        }

values.name: sceneQuery
values.comment: Queries the glTF file for scene by index.

values.name: update
values.comment:
Internal update function. Call it in your update something like this:

    GltfMsg msg_ ->
        Gltf.update
            { toMsg = GltfMsg
            , onComplete = GltfOnComplete
            }
            msg_
            model.gltf
            |> Tuple.mapFirst (\gltf -> { model | gltf = gltf })

The onComplete function will be called when the [query](Gltf#queries) is ready. Your application Msg type should handle these:

    type Msg
        = GltfMsg Gltf.Msg
        | GltfOnComplete (Result Gltf.Error Gltf.QueryResult)

In the _onComplete_ handler you can use the [content](Gltf#content) functions to get a tree of nodes, animations, cameras etc.
values.type: { toMsg : Gltf.Msg -> msg, onComplete : Result.Result Gltf.Error Gltf.QueryResult -> msg } -> Gltf.Msg -> Gltf.Gltf -> ( Gltf.Gltf, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

name: Gltf.Animation

unions.name: AnimatedBone
unions.comment: An animated bone used for skeletal/skinned animation
cases: { skinIndex : Basics.Int, inverseBindMatrix : Math.Matrix4.Mat4, joint : Math.Matrix4.Mat4 }

unions.name: AnimatedProperty
unions.comment:
The animated [Transforms](Gltf-Transform#Transform) of a [Node](Gltf-Node#Node) separated into TRS properties.

**NOTE:** Scale and weights are not supported at this point.

values.name: animatedBoneTransforms
values.comment: With the [Animations](Gltf-Animation#Animation) received from a [Gltf.QueryResult](Gltf#animations) and a [Skin](Gltf-Skin#Skin) this will give you [AnimatedBones](Gltf-Animation#AnimatedBone) that can be used to deform a mesh at a point in time.
values.type: Basics.Float -> List.List Gltf.Animation.Animation -> Gltf.Skin.Skin -> List.List Gltf.Animation.AnimatedBone

values.name: animatedProperties
values.comment: With the [Animations](Gltf-Animation#Animation) received from a [Gltf.QueryResult](Gltf#animations) this will give you [AnimatedProperties](Gltf-Animation#AnimatedProperty) to transform your nodes at a point in time.

values.name: name
values.comment: The user-defined name of the animation

name: Gltf.Camera
comment:
A camera’s projection as defined in the [glTF specification](https://registry.khronos.org/glTF/specs/2.0/glTF-2.0.html#reference-camera).

@docs Camera, Index, Projection, OrthographicCamera, PerspectiveCamera

unions.name: Index
unions.comment: Use this to get a [cameraByIndex](Gltf#cameraByIndex)

unions.name: Projection
unions.comment: Specifies if the camera uses a perspective or orthographic projection.

aliases.name: Camera
aliases.comment: A camera’s with a [Perspective or Orthographic projection](Gltf-Camera#Projection)
aliases.type: { name : Maybe.Maybe String.String, index : Gltf.Camera.Index, projection : Gltf.Camera.Projection }

aliases.name: OrthographicCamera
aliases.comment: An orthographic camera containing properties to create an orthographic projection matrix.
aliases.type: { xMag : Basics.Float, yMag : Basics.Float, zFar : Basics.Float, zNear : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: PerspectiveCamera
aliases.comment: A perspective camera containing properties to create a perspective projection matrix.
aliases.type: { yFov : Basics.Float, zNear : Basics.Float, aspectRatio : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, zFar : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float }

name: Gltf.Material
comment:
A material as defined in the [glTF specification](https://registry.khronos.org/glTF/specs/2.0/glTF-2.0.html#reference-material).

@docs Material, Index, TextureIndex, AlphaMode, BbrMetallicRoughness

unions.name: AlphaMode
unions.comment: AlphaMode of the material to convert to a [WebGL.Settings.Setting](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/webgl/latest/WebGL-Settings))

unions.name: Index
unions.comment: The index of the material.

unions.name: Material
unions.comment: A material assigned to a [Mesh](Gltf-Mesh#Mesh) contained in a [Node](Gltf-Node#Node). Use the properties/textures in the material to render it.
cases: { name : Maybe.Maybe String.String, index : Gltf.Material.Index, pbrMetallicRoughness : Gltf.Material.BbrMetallicRoughness, normalTexture : Maybe.Maybe Gltf.Material.TextureIndex, normalTextureScale : Basics.Float, occlusionTexture : Maybe.Maybe Gltf.Material.TextureIndex, occlusionTextureStrength : Basics.Float, emissiveTexture : Maybe.Maybe Gltf.Material.TextureIndex, emissiveFactor : Math.Vector3.Vec3, doubleSided : Basics.Bool, alphaMode : Gltf.Material.AlphaMode }

aliases.name: BbrMetallicRoughness
aliases.comment: The [Material PBR Metallic Roughness](https://registry.khronos.org/glTF/specs/2.0/glTF-2.0.html#reference-material-pbrmetallicroughness) of the material.
aliases.type: { baseColorFactor : Math.Vector4.Vec4, baseColorTexture : Maybe.Maybe Gltf.Material.TextureIndex, metallicFactor : Basics.Float, roughnessFactor : Basics.Float, metallicRoughnessTexture : Maybe.Maybe Gltf.Material.TextureIndex }

name: Gltf.Mesh

unions.name: Mesh
unions.comment:
The types of meshes as you will find in [elm-explorations/webgl](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/webgl/latest/WebGL#Meshes)

**NOTE:** only meshes with triangles are supported currently. Other ["modes"](https://registry.khronos.org/glTF/specs/2.0/glTF-2.0.html#_mesh_primitive_mode) will come later.
cases: ( List.List Gltf.Mesh.Vertex, List.List ( Basics.Int, Basics.Int, Basics.Int ) )

aliases.name: Joints
aliases.comment: The indices of the joints in the [skeleton](Gltf-Skin#Skeleton) the vertex is effected by.
aliases.type: { j1 : Basics.Int, j2 : Basics.Int, j3 : Basics.Int, j4 : Basics.Int }

aliases.name: Vertex
aliases.comment: The "attributes" of the Mesh. Map these into the attribute your shader(s) use.
aliases.type: { position : Math.Vector3.Vec3, normal : Maybe.Maybe Math.Vector3.Vec3, tangent : Maybe.Maybe Math.Vector4.Vec4, color : Maybe.Maybe Math.Vector4.Vec4, weights : Maybe.Maybe Math.Vector4.Vec4, joints : Maybe.Maybe Gltf.Mesh.Joints, texCoords : Maybe.Maybe Math.Vector2.Vec2 }

name: Gltf.Node

unions.name: Node
unions.comment: The types of [Nodes](Gltf#nodeTrees) returned as a [Tree](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/zwilias/elm-rosetree/latest/Tree#Tree) by a [query](Gltf#queries)

unions.name: Properties
unions.comment: The [Transform](Gltf-Transform#Transform) of the Node along with name and index.
cases: { nodeIndex : Gltf.NodeIndex.NodeIndex, nodeName : Maybe.Maybe String.String, transform : Gltf.Transform.Transform }

name: Gltf.NodeIndex
comment:
The identifier for a Node

@docs NodeIndex

unions.name: NodeIndex
unions.comment: The identifier for a [Node](Gltf-Node#Node) used to get its [Animations](Gltf-Animation#Animation) etc.

name: Gltf.Scene
comment:
A summary of a scene returned by [Gltf.scenes](Gltf#scenes).

@docs Scene, Index

unions.name: Index
unions.comment: The identifier for a Skin

aliases.name: Scene
aliases.type: { name : Maybe.Maybe String.String, index : Gltf.Scene.Index, default : Basics.Bool }

name: Gltf.Skin
comment:
The [Meshes](Gltf-Mesh#Mesh) of a _SkinnedMeshNode_ can be deformed/animated using a Skin.

@docs Index, Skin, Skeleton

unions.name: Index
unions.comment: The identifier for a Skin

unions.name: Skin
unions.comment: Joints and matrices defining a skin.
cases: { inverseBindMatrices : List.List Math.Matrix4.Mat4, joints : List.List Gltf.NodeIndex.NodeIndex, index : Gltf.Skin.Index, skeleton : Gltf.Skin.Skeleton }

name: Gltf.Transform
comment:
The [Nodes](Gltf-Node#Node) in glTF define their local [transformations](https://registry.khronos.org/glTF/specs/2.0/glTF-2.0.html#transformations) as either as a [matrix](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/linear-algebra/latest/Math-Matrix4#Mat4) or a combination of [translation](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/linear-algebra/latest/Math-Vector3#Vec3), [rotation](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/nphollon/geo3d/latest/Quaternion#Quaternion), and [scale](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/linear-algebra/latest/Math-Vector3#Vec3) (also known as TRS properties).

@docs Transform

unions.name: Transform
unions.comment: The local transform of a [Node](Gltf-Node#Node).
cases: { translation : Maybe.Maybe Math.Vector3.Vec3, rotation : Maybe.Maybe Quaternion.Quaternion, scale : Maybe.Maybe Math.Vector3.Vec3 }
